title: roebuckia cheilocarpa
intro: Roebuckia cheilocarpa, also known as Mormon tea or desert celery, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the borage family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Roebuckia cheilocarpa is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The plant's leaves and stems can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried for use in herbal supplements or as a culinary ingredient in traditional recipes.
